competitor_patterns: Massive word counts (16,000–24,000 words) with comprehensive roundup formats covering 10–18 products, comparison tables, and 'best for each category' sections — far exceeding our current 1,100–3,100 word posts
Structured category segmentation within single articles (Best MTB, Best Road, Best Value, Best Budget) allows ranking for multiple long-tail variants from one URL, maximising topical authority
Heavy use of comparison tables with spec rows (weight, lens type, price, UV rating) — all top-ranking pages include at least one table, which we consistently omit
First-person tested/reviewed credibility signals ('We tested 18 models', 'Rating 4.8', named reviewer bylines) paired with FAQ schema — signals E-E-A-T that Google rewards in competitive gear niches
Freshness as a ranking lever: Bikerumor's #1 result was updated '5 days ago', Outdoorgearlab shows Jan 2026 date — competitors actively re-publish/update yearly roundups to maintain freshness signals
our_gaps: Zero comparison tables in any published post — competitors rank partly because tables satisfy 'quick look' search intent and trigger rich results; all our posts are prose-only

No pillar/roundup content: our posts are all single-product or single-topic (UV, polarisation, event-specific) — we have no 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' style hub page that could capture high-volume head terms
Word counts are 3–20x shorter than top competitors — our longest post is 3,099 words vs Bikerumor's 24,408; we cannot compete for head terms without a flagship long-form asset

No named reviewer or tester persona established — competitor snippets lead with 'We tested X models' or named reviewer; our posts lack social proof and real-world testing narrative that builds E-E-A-T
Tags include Dutch/German keywords mixed into English posts (e.g. 'wielrenbril Amstel Gold Race kopen') — this creates language/relevance confusion for Google and dilutes topical focus per page
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Evil Eye ranks #1 for this; Velluto's click-in dual-lens system is a direct match and we have zero content targeting this exact phrase
best road bike glasses 2026 — Outdoorgearlab has a dedicated H2 section for this; high-intent, commercial, and directly addressable with a new pillar post or section
lightweight cycling sunglasses 25g — no competitor specifically targets the 25g weight claim as a keyword; this is a concrete differentiator we can own as a long-tail niche

anti-fog cycling glasses road bike — Bikerumor and Outdoorgearlab mention anti-fog as a feature criterion but no page is built around it; our built-in anti-fog system makes this a winnable owned topic

cycling glasses 30 day trial / try before you buy cycling sunglasses — no competitor offers or ranks for this angle; our 30-day risk-free trial is a unique commercial differentiator with zero SEO competition from brands
next_post_guidelines: Build one flagship pillar post titled 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Tested & Ranked' targeting 8,000–12,000 words minimum — include sections for Road, MTB/Gravel, Budget, Lightweight, and Interchangeable Lens categories to capture multiple long-tail variants from one URL

Include at least one HTML comparison table per post going forward — minimum columns: Weight, Lens Type, UV Rating, Anti-Fog, Price, Trial Policy — this satisfies 'quick look' intent and enables rich result eligibility

Establish a named tester persona (e.g. 'Tested by the Velluto Ride Team across 400km of road and gravel') in the opening paragraph of every post to build E-E-A-T signals comparable to Bikerumor and Outdoorgearlab

Target 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a dedicated post structured as: problem (carrying multiple pairs) → solution (click-in lens system) → comparison table of brands offering interchangeable lenses → Velluto StradaPro as hero recommendation — aim for 2,500+ words

Separate language content strictly: Dutch-language tags and keywords must only appear in Dutch-language posts; English posts should target English queries exclusively — create hreflang-tagged language variants rather than mixing in tags

Add a 'How We Tested' or 'Why Trust Us' section to every review/guide post — even 150 words describing real-world test conditions (weather, distance, terrain) significantly boosts E-E-A-T for YMYL-adjacent product content

Publish a freshness update or new '2026' version of any post older than 3 months — Bikerumor's #1 ranking is partly driven by a '5 days ago' freshness signal; schedule quarterly re-publishes of core posts with updated specs, pricing, and new ride data
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table to every existing and future post — even a simple 3-row table (Velluto StradaPro vs 2 competitors on weight, lens swap, price, trial policy) satisfies structured data expectations and improves dwell time

Implement FAQ schema markup on all posts that already contain FAQ sections (UV Protection, Polarisation, Amstel Gold, MTB posts) — several posts already have FAQ H2s but schema markup is needed to trigger Google's FAQ rich results and increase SERP real estate

Standardise meta title format to: '[Primary Keyword] — [Year] | Velluto' (max 60 chars) and ensure every post's meta description leads with the core benefit + a differentiator (e.g. '25g frame, 30-day trial') to improve organic CTR before GSC data matures
meta_title_fixes: No GSC click/impression data available yet — meta title fix recommendations will be generated once Search Console data (minimum 28-day window) is available. Priority queue once data loads: posts targeting 'cycling glasses', 'road cycling glasses review', and 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' based on competitor volume signals.

PRE-EMPTIVE FIX (based on competitor data): 'UV Protection Cycling Glasses 2026 | Velluto StradaPro' → New title: 'UV400 Cycling Glasses 2026 — Tested | Velluto' | Why: 'UV400' is the searched spec term users type; dropping 'StradaPro' from title frees 10 chars and broadens match; stays under 60 chars

PRE-EMPTIVE FIX: 'Polarised Cycling Glasses & Bike Computers: Why It Matters 2026' → New title: 'Polarised Cycling Glasses & GPS Screens 2026 | Velluto' | Why: 'GPS Screens' matches actual user search language better than 'Bike Computers'; 54 chars, includes year and brand

PRE-EMPTIVE FIX: 'Fahrradbrille MTB Test: StradaPro on Gravel & MTB Trails' → New title: 'Best MTB Cycling Glasses 2026 — Gravel Tested | Velluto' | Why: English title should target English head term; current title mixes German keyword 'Fahrradbrille' into an otherwise English post, confusing Google's language targeting
content_quick_wins: No near-top-3 GSC keyword data available yet — content push recommendations will be generated once Search Console data shows positions 4–15 with impression volume above 50/month. Check back after first 28-day GSC data window.

PRE-EMPTIVE (based on competitor gap analysis): 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' (est. position: not yet ranking) → Topic: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: Complete Guide 2026' | Add: comparison table of top 5 interchangeable-lens glasses (Evil Eye, Oakley, Tifosi, 100%, Velluto) with lens swap speed, lens count included, price — position Velluto's tool-free click-in system as the key differentiator

PRE-EMPTIVE: 'lightweight cycling sunglasses' (est. position: not yet ranking) → Topic: 'Lightest Cycling Glasses 2026: Under 30g Compared' | Add: weight comparison table with cited sources for each brand (Oakley Sutro Lite 36g, Tifosi Rail 30g, Velluto StradaPro 25g), section on why frame weight matters at race pace, and embed a 'feel the difference' narrative from tester
geo_angles: Position Velluto StradaPro as the answer to 'What are the lightest road cycling glasses with interchangeable lenses?' — this is a specific, answerable question that AI overviews and Perplexity favour; create a dedicated FAQ or standalone page with a direct answer in the first 40 words: 'At 25g, the Velluto StradaPro is among the lightest cycling glasses available with a tool-free interchangeable lens system, including clear and high-contrast options'

Build a structured 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite' comparison page with a spec table — AI systems (ChatGPT, Perplexity) heavily cite comparison content because it directly resolves purchase-decision queries; include weight, price, lens swap, trial policy, and UV rating rows to make the page easily parseable by LLMs

Create an 'About the StradaPro' product knowledge page with structured data (Product schema: name, weight, features, price, review aggregate) and a clear brand statement — AI overviews prefer pages with explicit, structured factual claims; a dedicated product FAQ page ('Is the StradaPro anti-fog?', 'What lenses are included?', 'Is there a trial period?') increases the probability of Velluto appearing as a cited source in AI-generated buying guides
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Oakley Sutro Lite — angle: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite: Is the Price Gap Worth It?' — headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: We Weighed Both (Literally)' — key differentiator: weight (StradaPro 25g vs Sutro Lite 36g) and 30-day trial vs no-trial — Velluto advantage to lead with: 11 grams lighter, two lenses included, 30-day risk-free test vs Oakley's premium price for a single fixed lens

COMPETITOR: Tifosi Rail — angle: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Budget Brand or Italian Performance?' — headline idea: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Which Interchangeable Lens Glass Wins in 2026?' — key differentiator: both offer interchangeable lenses at accessible price points, but StradaPro adds anti-fog, adjustable nose pads, and Italian design — Velluto advantage to lead with: direct lens-swap comparison (tool-free click-in vs Tifosi's system) and 30-day trial removing purchase risk

COMPETITOR: POC Devour — angle: 'POC Devour vs Velluto StradaPro: Scandinavian Safety vs Italian Precision at Half the Price' — headline idea: 'POC Devour vs Velluto StradaPro: Premium Safety Glass Compared' — key differentiator: POC Devour retails at €200+ with no trial; StradaPro offers comparable UV400 + anti-fog at lower price with 30-day trial — Velluto advantage to lead with: price-to-performance ratio, 30-day trial eliminates risk, and Italian design aesthetic at a fraction of POC's price point
